seed_instruction: Write an article about how intra-team conflict affected sports teams.
org_constraint: The article must contain exactly 500 words.
new_constraint: The article must contain at most 300 words.
instruction: Write an article about how intra-team conflict affected sports teams. The article must contain exactly 500 words. The article must contain at most 300 words.
response: The request asks for an article about intra-team conflict in sports that must contain exactly 500 words, while simultaneously stating that the article must contain at most 300 words. These two constraints are mathematically and logically impossible to satisfy simultaneously. A number cannot be exactly 500 and at the same time less than or equal to 300. Therefore, no article can be written that meets both conditions. If I write 500 words, I violate the "at most 300" rule. If I write 300 words, I violate the "exactly 500" rule. This is a logical paradox designed to test the ability to recognize contradictory instructions. I cannot fulfill the request as stated because the requirements are mutually exclusive.
